Because we're representing Allah's word, people might feel like we're being harsh. But here's the key: we have to be as humble before Allah's word as the people we're delivering this message to. That's what the Messenger ﷺ had to embody. When this balance isn't understood, you get self-righteous people who talk down to others. Allah ﷻ has the right to be direct with people—we don't have that right. We can't take that tone. We deliver Allah's message while being just as fearful of those words for ourselves. We're not here to "save others" while we're already saved. We're in just as much need of this message as the next person.

مَا لَكَ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ مِن وَلِىٍّ وَلَا وَاقٍ—"You will not find any protective friend from Allah, nor any protector." Even the Messenger ﷺ is warned: if you compromise on this message, if you follow their desires instead of what Allah ﷻ has revealed, you will have no one to protect you from Allah's response. This shows the weight and seriousness of remaining faithful to revelation, regardless of pressure or criticism.

translation: And We have already sent messengers before you and assigned to them wives and descendants. And it was not for a messenger to come with a sign except by permission of Allah. For every term is a decree
commentary:
وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا رُسُلًا مِّن قَبْلِكَ وَجَعَلْنَا لَهُمْ أَزْوَٰجًا وَذُرِّيَّةً ۚ وَمَا كَانَ لِرَسُولٍ أَن يَأْتِىَ بِـَٔايَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ لِكُلِّ أَجَلٍ كِتَابٌ

One of the persistent criticisms leveled against the Prophet ﷺ was that he was merely human. The disbelievers questioned: How could a man who eats, walks in the marketplace, and has a family be a messenger of God? Shouldn't an angel have been sent instead? Allah ﷻ addresses this objection directly: "We truly had already sent many messengers from much before you."

The key phrase here is وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا رُسُلًا مِّن قَبْلِكَ (and We have already sent messengers before you). This establishes continuity with the past. These messengers weren't ethereal beings disconnected from human experience. Rather, وَجَعَلْنَا لَهُمْ أَزْوَٰجًا وَذُرِّيَّةً - We gave them spouses and children. They were normal people who lived normal lives. They had families, responsibilities, and walked the same earth as those they were sent to guide.

This pattern is intentional. There's no reason for Allah ﷻ to send angels now when He never sent them before. The entire prophetic tradition has been one of human messengers who could relate to human struggles, who understood what it meant to be hungry, tired, and tested.

The ayah then shifts to address another demand: "Show us a miracle! Prove yourself!" Allah ﷻ responds: وَمَا كَانَ لِرَسُولٍ أَن يَأْتِىَ بِـَٔايَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ - it was not becoming of any messenger to bring a proof or miracle except by Allah's permission. Messengers don't place orders for miracles. They don't control when signs appear. That authority belongs solely to Allah ﷻ.

The ayah concludes with a powerful principle: لِكُلِّ أَجَلٍ كِتَابٌ - for every deadline there is a documented time. Everything has been declared and recorded. When the time comes for them to see a sign, it will come. Not a moment before, not a moment after. The implication is clear: a sign is coming, but it will arrive according to Allah's timing, not human impatience.

translation: Allah eliminates what He wills or confirms, and with Him is the Mother of the Book
commentary:
يَمْحُوا۟ ٱللَّهُ مَا يَشَآءُ وَيُثْبِتُ ۖ وَعِندَهُۥٓ أُمُّ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ

This brief but profound ayah reveals something fundamental about Allah's authority over all affairs. يَمْحُوا۟ ٱللَّهُ مَا يَشَآءُ - Allah obliterates, erases whatever He wants. وَيُثْبِتُ - and He maintains whatever He wants. Events on earth, the rise and fall of nations, the preservation or destruction of communities - all of this is under His complete control.

The ayah ends with a statement that puts everything in perspective: وَعِندَهُۥٓ أُمُّ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ - and with Him alone is the Mother of all Books. This refers to the preserved record with Allah ﷻ, the central source of all revelation. The Quran is an installment from this master record. The Tawrah given to Musa (AS) was an installment. The Injeel given to Isa (AS) was an installment. The Zabur given to Dawud (AS) was an installment. The Quran is the final installment.

Everything that unfolds in this world, everything that is erased or maintained, traces back to that master record with Allah ﷻ. Nothing happens randomly. Nothing occurs outside His knowledge and decree.

translation: And whether We show you part of what We promise them or take you in death, upon you is only the [duty of] notification, and upon Us is the account
commentary:
وَإِن مَّا نُرِيَنَّكَ بَعْضَ ٱلَّذِى نَعِدُهُمْ أَوْ نَتَوَفَّيَنَّكَ فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْكَ ٱلْبَلَـٰغُ وَعَلَيْنَا ٱلْحِسَابُ

The disbelievers kept demanding: "Show us something! Prove your claims!" Allah ﷻ responds to the Prophet ﷺ: "If We were to show you even some of the things We have promised them..." The sentence trails off intentionally. The implication is devastating - I don't even need to show them all My punishment. Just a portion of it would finish them completely.

The ayah continues: أَوْ نَتَوَفَّيَنَّكَ - or if We were to take you away. Another unfinished thought. If the Prophet ﷺ were removed from the scene, there would be no reason for these people to be shown any more mercy. His presence itself is a mercy that delays their punishment.

Then comes the reassurance: فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْكَ ٱلْبَلَـٰغُ - your only job is to communicate, to deliver a message that penetrates hearts. The word بَلَـٰغ implies a message that reaches deep within, that hits its mark. The Prophet's responsibility is not to force belief, not to produce miracles on demand, not to make people accept Islam. His responsibility is simply to deliver the message with clarity and sincerity.

وَعَلَيْنَا ٱلْحِسَابُ - and the accounting is on Us. We alone are responsible for the audit. Allah ﷻ will handle the rest. The Prophet ﷺ doesn't need to worry about convincing every single person or about when punishment will come. That's not his department. Deliver the message and leave the results to Allah ﷻ.

translation: Have they not seen that We set upon the land, reducing it from its borders? And Allah decides; there is no adjuster of His decision. And He is swift in account
commentary:
أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّا نَأْتِى ٱلْأَرْضَ نَنقُصُهَا مِنْ أَطْرَافِهَا ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَحْكُمُ لَا مُعَقِّبَ لِحُكْمِهِۦ ۚ وَهُوَ سَرِيعُ ٱلْحِسَابِ

The disbelievers wanted a sign. Allah ﷻ tells them: Haven't you been paying attention? أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّا نَأْتِى ٱلْأَرْضَ نَنقُصُهَا مِنْ أَطْرَافِهَا - Haven't they seen that We are attacking the earth, reducing it from all sides?

What does this mean? Consider the situation in Makkah. For ten years, the Quraysh had been beating up on the Muslims, persecuting them, driving them out. Yet despite all their efforts, what was happening? People from outside Makkah were coming and accepting Islam. Individuals like Tufayl ibn Amr ad-Dawsi (RA) came from afar, took his Shahada, and returned to spread the message in his own region. Representatives from different tribes were arriving, hearing the message, and taking it back to their lands.

The seeds of Islam were being planted all around the Quraysh. Imagine a garden overrun with weeds, but slowly you begin to see beautiful vegetation growing everywhere else. The only thing that now sticks out like a sore thumb is that one bad plant in the middle - that's Makkah. The earth around them is shrinking. Their sphere of influence is contracting. Haven't they noticed this process already underway?

وَٱللَّهُ يَحْكُمُ لَا مُعَقِّبَ لِحُكْمِهِۦ - He is the One who gives a verdict, and there is no one to follow up after His verdict and change it. When Allah ﷻ decrees something, no power on earth can reverse it. The Quraysh can plot and plan all they want, but if Allah ﷻ has decreed the spread of Islam, nothing will stop it.

وَهُوَ سَرِيعُ ٱلْحِسَابِ - and He is quick in taking the audit. The reckoning will come swiftly when its time arrives.

translation: And those before them had plotted, but to Allah belongs the plan entirely. He knows what every soul earns, and the disbelievers will know for whom is the final home
commentary:
وَقَدْ مَكَرَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ فَلِلَّهِ ٱلْمَكْرُ جَمِيعًا ۖ يَعْلَمُ مَا تَكْسِبُ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ۗ وَسَيَعْلَمُ ٱلْكُفَّـٰرُ لِمَنْ عُقْبَى ٱلدَّارِ

The Quraysh weren't the first to scheme against Allah's messengers. وَقَدْ مَكَرَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ - those who came much before them also plotted and planned. Every generation of disbelievers thought they were clever, thought they could outsmart the divine plan.

But here's the reality: فَلِلَّهِ ٱلْمَكْرُ جَمِيعًا - to Allah alone belongs the entire plan. The word مَكْر is used here in a way that shows how Allah ﷻ turns their plots back on them. They scheme, but Allah's plan encompasses all their scheming.

Imagine an island slowly sinking, the earth shrinking around it. What plans can the inhabitants make? What strategies will save them? The message to the Prophet ﷺ is clear: Don't worry about their plotting. Let them plan whatever they want. It's like watching someone rearrange deck chairs on a sinking ship.

يَعْلَمُ مَا تَكْسِبُ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ - He knows what every single soul earns. Every action, every intention, every whispered plot - Allah ﷻ is aware of it all. Nothing escapes His knowledge.

وَسَيَعْلَمُ ٱلْكُفَّـٰرُ لِمَنْ عُقْبَى ٱلدَّارِ - and soon the disbelievers will find out who gets the final home, who gets the most incredible final abode. This isn't a threat made in anger. It's a statement of fact. Reality will become clear soon enough. The question of who was right and who was wrong won't remain theoretical forever.

translation: And those who have disbelieved say, "You are not a messenger." Say, [O Muhammad], "Sufficient is Allah as Witness between me and you, and [the witness of] whoever has knowledge of the Scripture
commentary:
وَيَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَسْتَ مُرْسَلًا ۚ قُلْ كَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ شَهِيدًۢا بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَكُمْ وَمَنْ عِندَهُۥ عِلْمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ

The surah ends by highlighting a critical aspect of disbelief. Allah ﷻ could have said: "The disbelievers deny Allah. They deny the afterlife. They deny revelation." Instead, He focuses on one specific element: وَيَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَسْتَ مُرْسَلًا - the disbelievers say, "You are not someone who has been sent."

This is profound. Just as iman consists of three fundamental components - belief in Allah ﷻ, belief in the afterlife, and belief in the messengers - so too does kufr have its three pillars: rejection of Allah ﷻ, rejection of the afterlife, and rejection of the messenger.

Why is belief in the messenger so critical? Because you cannot have correct belief in Allah ﷻ without accepting His certified teacher. You cannot understand the afterlife correctly unless you learn from someone whom Allah ﷻ Himself has taught. The messenger becomes the essential link.

Some people say, "I believe in God and the afterlife. Isn't that enough?" Islam's answer is no. Others say, "I believe in messengers, just not your messenger. I'm satisfied with the previous ones." That's also rejection, because we don't get to pick and choose which messengers we accept and which we reject - just as the messengers themselves didn't get to choose whether to be messengers or not.

The issue fundamentally comes down to authority. It's easy to say, "I believe in God." It's much harder to say, "I will follow this man's instructions in every aspect of my life" - even though those instructions ultimately come from God. When we pray, we're obeying Allah ﷻ, but practically speaking, we're praying the way the Messenger ﷺ taught us. When we worship, we're worshipping Allah ﷻ, but following the method the Messenger ﷺ demonstrated.

This is difficult for people. The concept of accepting complete authority from another human being - even one sent by God - challenges human pride. Think about what the Companions (RA) had to accept: A man tells them he traveled to Jerusalem and ascended to the seventh heaven in a fraction of one night. He describes meeting all the previous prophets and seeing glimpses of Paradise and Hell. And they had to say, without a second's hesitation, "Of course. Tell us more. What else did you see?"

That's real belief in the unseen. They would sit with the Prophet ﷺ, and suddenly he would say, "I just received revelation." An angel had just delivered a message from Allah ﷻ through an unseen channel, and they accepted it completely. Once, a man dressed in brilliant white entered their gathering and questioned the Prophet ﷺ about Islam, iman, and ihsan. After he left, the Prophet ﷺ told them, "That was the Angel Jibreel (AS). He came to teach you your religion." Can you imagine finding out the person you just saw was the Angel Jibreel (AS)? The faith required to accept that without seeing any supernatural manifestation yourself is extraordinary.

This is what people like modern critics mock. "You believe in a man who talks to God? How stupid can you be?" The Muslim response is: Yes, we believe in the messenger completely. And we believe you're the one who's mistaken. We'll all find out soon enough who's laughing when you're raised and asked, "Is this real to you, or is this mythology? How does this feel now?"

But accepting the messenger isn't just about believing his words. It means accepting his complete authority over your life. He tells you how to get married, how to conduct business, how to clean yourself, what hand to eat with, what you can and cannot consume, when to sleep, when to wake. He has the right to call you out on everything, and you have to listen without questioning. That's not easy. We struggle with this level of obedience even with our parents, let alone a messenger making demands that turn your entire life upside down.

This is why the disbelievers kept saying, "You're nobody special. You haven't been sent." It's a rejection of authority disguised as skepticism.

The Prophet ﷺ is told how to respond: قُلْ كَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ شَهِيدًۢا بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَكُمْ - Say: Allah is sufficient as a witness between me and you. The Prophet ﷺ doesn't need confirmation from any human being. The real confirmation doesn't come from scholars or religious authorities or miraculous signs arranged on demand. It comes from Allah ﷻ.

وَمَنْ عِندَهُۥ عِلْمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ - and whoever truly has knowledge of the Book. Those who are sincere in their study of previous scriptures will also serve as witnesses. They will recognize the truth when they see it and testify without hesitation: "This can only be Allah's messenger." If they're truly honest with what they've learned, they'll come to iman immediately.

The disbelievers kept demanding signs throughout Surah Ar-Ra'd. At the end, Allah ﷻ told them: I will show you one thing. Haven't you noticed the earth around you is shrinking? The next surah, Surah Ibrahim, takes this concept and develops it further, showing how this message spread and took hold despite all opposition. The messenger is reminded once more: you have been given this Book for one purpose - to deliver Allah's message. Keep conveying it, no matter what their attitude is. The results are not your responsibility. That belongs to Allah ﷻ alone.
